message:
fix(algorithms): correct assortativity formulas, mixing matrix symmetry, and min-cost flow demand convention

- attribute_mixing_dict: always count both (u,v) and (v,u) for undirected
  graphs instead of skipping when u_val == v_val
- attribute_assortativity: replace row/col sum shortcut with full e@e matrix
  product per Newman (2003) Eq. (2); return NaN instead of 1.0 for
  degenerate denominator
- numeric_assortativity_coefficient: rewrite using normalized mixing matrix
  and marginal-based Pearson correlation (Newman 2003 Eq. 21) instead of
  raw edge-sum formula
- min_cost_flow / network_simplex: fix demand sign convention to match
  NetworkX (negative = supply/source, positive = demand/sink)
- digraph MultiDiGraph.to_undirected: preserve edge keys via
  add_edge_with_key_and_attrs instead of add_edge_with_attrs
- Update test_parity_comprehensive to match corrected demand convention

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -4492,8 +4492,8 @@ def min_cost_flow(G, demand='demand', capacity='capacity', weight='weight'):
     Uses the successive shortest paths algorithm with Bellman-Ford.
 
     Each node may have a ``demand`` attribute:
-    - Positive demand = supply (source)
-    - Negative demand = demand (sink)
+    - Negative demand = supply (source, has excess flow to send)
+    - Positive demand = demand (sink, needs flow)
     - Zero or missing = transshipment node
 
     Parameters
@@ -4546,11 +4546,11 @@ def min_cost_flow(G, demand='demand', capacity='capacity', weight='weight'):
         flow.setdefault(u, {})[v] = 0
 
     # Build residual graph and run successive shortest paths
-    # Create a super-source and super-sink
-    sources = [n for n in nodes if node_demand[n] > 0]
-    sinks = [n for n in nodes if node_demand[n] < 0]
-    remaining_supply = {n: node_demand[n] for n in sources}
-    remaining_demand = {n: -node_demand[n] for n in sinks}
+    # NetworkX convention: negative demand = supply, positive = demand
+    sources = [n for n in nodes if node_demand[n] < 0]
+    sinks = [n for n in nodes if node_demand[n] > 0]
+    remaining_supply = {n: -node_demand[n] for n in sources}
+    remaining_demand = {n: node_demand[n] for n in sinks}
 
     # Successive shortest paths: augment along shortest cost path
     for _ in range(n * n):  # upper bound on iterations

@@ -183,8 +183,8 @@ def test_min_cost_flow_rejects_networkx_unfeasible_demand_signs(self):
 
     def test_network_simplex(self):
         D = fnx.DiGraph()
-        D.add_node(0, demand=2)
-        D.add_node(1, demand=-2)
+        D.add_node(0, demand=-2)
+        D.add_node(1, demand=2)
         D.add_edge(0, 1, capacity=10, weight=3)
         cost, flow = fnx.network_simplex(D)
         assert cost == 6.0
